verses.text: A voice bids the angels 'pour out the vials.' The first: a grievous sore on those with the beast's mark. First vial. Judgment falls on the beast's worshippers.
A voice bids the angels 'pour out the vials.' The first: a grievous sore on those with the beast's mark. First vial. Judgment falls on the beast's worshippers.
The second turns the sea to blood, every living thing dying; the third makes rivers and fountains blood. Waters of blood. The world's waters answer for shed blood.
The second turns the sea to blood, every living thing dying; the third makes rivers and fountains blood. Waters of blood. The world's waters answer for shed blood.
The angel says, 'Thou art righteous, O Lord... for they have shed the blood of saints'; the altar agrees, 'true and righteous are thy judgments.' Just. God's judgments are wholly right.
The angel says, 'Thou art righteous, O Lord... for they have shed the blood of saints'; the altar agrees, 'true and righteous are thy judgments.' Just. God's judgments are wholly right.
The angel says, 'Thou art righteous, O Lord... for they have shed the blood of saints'; the altar agrees, 'true and righteous are thy judgments.' Just. God's judgments are wholly right.
The fourth scorches men with fire of the sun; yet they 'blasphemed the name of God... and repented not.' No repentance. Pain only hardens the rebel heart.
The fourth scorches men with fire of the sun; yet they 'blasphemed the name of God... and repented not.' No repentance. Pain only hardens the rebel heart.
The fifth fills the beast's kingdom with darkness; men gnaw their tongues, blaspheme, and 'repented not of their deeds.' Darkness. Still they will not turn to God.
The fifth fills the beast's kingdom with darkness; men gnaw their tongues, blaspheme, and 'repented not of their deeds.' Darkness. Still they will not turn to God.
The sixth dries Euphrates for the kings of the east; unclean spirits gather the kings 'to the battle of that great day of God.' Gathering. Demons draw the world to Armageddon.
The sixth dries Euphrates for the kings of the east; unclean spirits gather the kings 'to the battle of that great day of God.' Gathering. Demons draw the world to Armageddon.
The sixth dries Euphrates for the kings of the east; unclean spirits gather the kings 'to the battle of that great day of God.' Gathering. Demons draw the world to Armageddon.
'Behold, I come as a thief. Blessed is he that watcheth.' They are gathered to 'Armageddon.' Watch. The Lord's sudden coming calls for readiness.
'Behold, I come as a thief. Blessed is he that watcheth.' They are gathered to 'Armageddon.' Watch. The Lord's sudden coming calls for readiness.
The seventh vial; a great voice cries, 'It is done.' Thunders and 'a great earthquake, such as was not since men were.' It is done. God's wrath reaches its end.
The seventh vial; a great voice cries, 'It is done.' Thunders and 'a great earthquake, such as was not since men were.' It is done. God's wrath reaches its end.
Great Babylon is remembered, cities fall, islands flee, and great hail falls; men 'blasphemed God' still. The end. Even final judgment leaves the wicked unrepentant.
